title: Inverse Kinematics
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Inverse Kinematics: Inverse kinematics: find joint angles q that achieve desired end-effector pose T_desired. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.ik.jacobian_pseudo_inverse…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~200; singularity_proximity, joint_limit_approach set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: FK(q) = T_desired; solved iteratively via Jacobian J: dq = J^+ * de
E.world_state_x: joint angle vector q in R ** n satisfying FK(q) = T_desired
E.observation_y: desired end-effector pose (position + orientation) as input

W.regime: Existence of the recovered joint_angle_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 200); singularity_proximity dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Deterministic sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.solver_class: classical [Jacobian_pseudo_inverse or DLS or FABRIK or analytical_closed_form]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: position error < 0.1 mm in 50 iterations for non-singular config. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~200. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |singularity_proximity|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(n ** 3 * N_iter) per IK solution per iteration
